and um all right so the cellulose doesn't bring acidity um so when they're processing the cellulose they dissolve it in acid first so that it breaks down that large chunk of whatever it may be so say some cotton pulp there and then they react it to to as a functional groups that allow it to swell in the way we want and then they neutralize it to get rid of of that so by the time we get it back for our paint um it's a pH neutral material that then is added into the paint and when it meets uh the higher pH the alkalinity of the paint it expands and swells and activates in that environment so yeah
